revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:チブサン古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Chibusan - Obusan Kofun | native_name =チブサン・オブサン古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Chibusan Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Chibusan Kofun | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chibusan - Obusan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.029667|N|130.669389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Chibusan-Obusan Kofun|qid=Q134563276|1=Chibusan-Obusan Kofun}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|01|50|N|130|40|03|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{nihongo|'''Chibusan Kofun - Obusan Kofun'''|チブサン・オブサン古墳||qq=}} are a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Jyō neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|qid=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The Chibusan Kofun was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922 and the Obusan Kofun was added to the designation in 1999.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211510|title=チブサン・オブサン古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The '''Chibusan Kofu'''n is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the right bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River in the northern part of the prefecture, at the eastern end of the Hiraogi Plateau. The Chibusan Kofun has length of 45 meters, wth a 24-meter diameter posterior circular part, and a 15.7 meter wide anterior rectangular portion. It has a multi-chambered horizontal entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]], with a front and rear chambers, and an antechamber to the south. It is a [[decorated kofun]] with colored murals of geometric patterns and figures wearing crowns on the inner walls and ceiling of the burial chamber. The upper row has seven white circular patterns, and the lower row has a figure wearing a crown with arms and legs spread and a triangular pattern to the right of it, painted in white, and the rest is painted in red. The side stones on the front are mainly painted with triangular and diamond patterns, with a circular pattern in the center of the front, and are painted in three colors: red, white, and blue. The tumulus is believed to have been constructed around the mid-6th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus has been open since ancient times, and the present location of any excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are unknown, but cylindrical and figurative ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been excavated in preliminary surveys. A stone figure in the shape of octopus once stood at the boundary between the front and rear mounds. It is currently kept at the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. The nearby '''Obusan Kofun''' is a circular {{nihongo|''en-fun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}} tumulus with a diameter of 22 meters surrounded by a moat about 4 meters wide. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' or ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found. It was built in the late 6th century and had post-burial sites until the early 7th century. It has a stone burial chamber with a total length of approximately 8.5 meters from the main gate to the inner wall, and if the 8 meters of the vestibule are added, it comes to 16.5 meters. A series of red triangular patterns were found on the partition stone that marks the inner chamber's coffin platform, and a small red ridge or shield was also found on the inner wall, but these were only traces and could not be read by the naked eye. It is highly likely that the inner chamber originally had richly decorated murals. Horse equipment with a base of iron and gold and copper plating has been excavated. <ref name="Shiseki"/> The kofun are the central portion of the Kumamoto Prefectural Fudoki-no-Oka Park. The tumulus is about 17 kilometers northeast of {{ill|Shin-Tamana Station|en|Shin-Tamana Station|qid=Q1136695|lt=Shin-Tamana Station|1=Shin-Tamana Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Shinkansen|en|Kyushu Shinkansen|qid=Q11370254|lt=Kyushu Shinkansen|1=Kyushu Shinkansen}}. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.city.yamaga.kumamoto.jp/kiji00390/index.html Yamaga City home page]
Chibusan-Obusan Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
